title: autocue/perf.py with perf_span context manager + ring buffer
description: Lightweight in-process performance instrumentation. perf_span(name) records start/stop wall-clock; results stream into a fixed-size ring buffer. Used by all SSE endpoints (TASK-046) to capture per-request latency.
acceptanceCriteria: Module maintains a ring buffer of last 1000 (name, start_ts, duration_ms) tuples.
perf_span is a no-op when AUTOCUE_PERF env var is unset (zero overhead path).
When AUTOCUE_PERF=1, samples 100% of spans; AUTOCUE_PERF_SAMPLE_RATE=0.1 samples 10%.
Tests in tests/test_perf.py cover: enable/disable, sampling rate, ring buffer eviction.
Span records p50/p95/p99 helpers: get_stats(name) -> {count, p50, p95, p99}.

steps.details: autocue/perf.py: imports time, os, threading, collections.deque, random. Module-level _enabled = os.environ.get('AUTOCUE_PERF', '0') == '1'; _sample_rate = float(os.environ.get('AUTOCUE_PERF_SAMPLE_RATE', '1.0')); _buffer = deque(maxlen=1000); _lock = threading.Lock().

@contextmanager def perf_span(name): if not _enabled or random.random() > _sample_rate: yield; return. start = time.perf_counter(); yield; dur = (time.perf_counter() - start) * 1000; with _lock: _buffer.append((name, start, dur)).
Filter _buffer by name; compute p50/p95/p99 from durations using statistics.quantiles or numpy.percentile. Return {count, p50, p95, p99} or None if no samples.
tests/test_perf.py: enable_perf monkeypatches _enabled = True; spans capture into buffer; get_stats returns correct percentiles; buffer evicts at >1000 entries.

technicalNotes: time.perf_counter is monotonic + high-resolution - the right choice over time.time.
The 'zero overhead when disabled' branch is critical - perf_span will be sprinkled across hot paths.
